# Provider Support Reference

<!-- Generated by `cd engine && go run ./cmd/supportdoc -write`. Do not edit by hand. -->

Piper is a deterministic local approximation. Hosted CI remains the final source of truth.

Statuses: `supported-local`, `emulated`, `partial`, `validation-only`, `unsupported`, and `requires-consent`.

Runtime dispositions: `execute`, `emulate`, `inspect-only`, `reject`, and `consent`.

This document is generated from 72 registry records. Each row includes the parser contract, runtime behavior, hosted difference, security note, fallback, and related test evidence.

## Common behavior

| Category / feature | Status / runtime | Parser contract | Local behavior | Hosted difference | Security / fallback / tests |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="common-job-dependency-graph"></a>Core — Job dependency graph (`common.graph`) | `supported-local` / `execute` | normalized needs and dependsOn edges | Piper builds a deterministic graph and rejects missing dependencies and cycles. | Provider-specific scheduling semantics are represented only where explicitly documented. | No pipeline code is executed while building the graph. Fallback: Correct missing or cyclic dependencies. Tests: [engine/internal/pipeline/graph/graph_test.go](../engine/internal/pipeline/graph/graph_test.go) |
| <a id="common-workflow-discovery-and-yaml-parsing"></a>Core — Workflow discovery and YAML parsing (`common.workflow.discovery`) | `supported-local` / `execute` | recognized provider files are loaded from the repository | Piper discovers and parses recognized local pipeline files. | Hosted providers may discover additional configuration through repository settings or remote composition. | YAML is treated as untrusted input and is not executed during discovery. Fallback: Use a documented provider filename and valid YAML. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go), [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go), [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="common-local-runner-approximation"></a>Runtime — Local runner approximation (`common.local-runner`) | `partial` / `execute` | requested runner metadata is retained | Jobs run in local Linux Docker containers selected by Piper. | Hosted images, tool caches, operating systems, identity, and machine configuration are not reproduced. | Containers are not a perfect sandbox and may access configured workspace and network resources. Fallback: Perform final validation on the hosted provider. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/runtime_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/runtime_test.go) |
| <a id="common-service-containers"></a>Runtime — Service containers (`common.services`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | common image, environment, aliases, ports, and startup timeout fields | Services run on an isolated per-job Docker network with health/startup checks. | Provider networking, DNS, option parsing, and lifecycle details can differ. | Services execute untrusted images and may expose configured host ports. Fallback: Use explicit images, aliases, health checks, and local-safe ports. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/services_integration_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/services_integration_test.go) |
| <a id="common-shell-step-execution"></a>Runtime — Shell step execution (`common.shell`) | `partial` / `execute` | provider shell/script steps are normalized | Shell steps execute in the selected job container using Bash or supported PowerShell handling. | Shell defaults, installed tools, profiles, and operating-system behavior can differ. | Pipeline scripts are arbitrary code with workspace access. Fallback: Choose a compatible image and explicit supported shell. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/runtime_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/runtime_test.go) |
| <a id="common-local-artifacts"></a>Storage — Local artifacts (`common.artifacts`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | common artifact declarations and built-in handlers | Artifacts are copied into Piper-managed local storage with workspace path checks. | Cloud retention, permissions, compression, naming, and cross-run behavior are not reproduced. | Artifact paths are restricted to the prepared workspace and symlinks are rejected. Fallback: Inspect or export the local artifact and verify hosted behavior separately. Tests: [engine/internal/artifacts/store_test.go](../engine/internal/artifacts/store_test.go) |
| <a id="common-local-caches"></a>Storage — Local caches (`common.caches`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | common cache keys, paths, restore keys, and policies | Caches use Piper-managed per-repository local storage. | Provider scope, immutability, eviction, versioning, and backend behavior differ. | Cache paths are workspace-restricted; restored content remains untrusted. Fallback: Clear local caches or verify cache behavior on hosted CI. Tests: [engine/internal/caches/store_test.go](../engine/internal/caches/store_test.go) |
| <a id="common-step-with-multiple-executable-forms"></a>Validation — Step with multiple executable forms (`common.ambiguous-step`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | provider step mappings with multiple run, action, task, checkout, or template forms are detected | An ambiguous step is rejected instead of executing one form and silently ignoring another. | Hosted providers normally reject mutually exclusive executable keys during validation. | Rejecting avoids executing a different command path than the author intended. Fallback: Keep exactly one executable form in each step. Tests: [engine/internal/pipeline/validation/validation_test.go](../engine/internal/pipeline/validation/validation_test.go), [engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go), [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="common-job-without-executable-steps"></a>Validation — Job without executable steps (`common.empty-job`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | jobs with no normalized steps are retained for diagnostics | A job with no locally executable steps is rejected instead of being reported successful. | A hosted provider may reject the configuration or obtain steps from a composition feature Piper does not resolve. | Rejecting prevents a missing template, include, or parser path from producing false success. Fallback: Add a supported step, resolve the configuration into a local fixture, or validate the job on hosted CI. Tests: [engine/internal/pipeline/validation/validation_test.go](../engine/internal/pipeline/validation/validation_test.go), [engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go), [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go), [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="common-empty-or-unknown-executable-step"></a>Validation — Empty or unknown executable step (`common.empty-step`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | step is retained for diagnostics | A step without a recognized executable form is rejected with a structured support error. | A hosted provider may reject the YAML earlier or support another step type. | Rejecting unknown execution prevents false-success results. Fallback: Use a supported run, action, or task form. Tests: [engine/internal/pipeline/validation/validation_test.go](../engine/internal/pipeline/validation/validation_test.go) |

## GitHub Actions

| Category / feature | Status / runtime | Parser contract | Local behavior | Hosted difference | Security / fallback / tests |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="github-artifact-and-cache-actions"></a>Actions — Artifact and cache actions (`github.builtin-storage-action`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | upload-artifact, download-artifact, and cache inputs used locally | Supported inputs use Piper-managed local artifact and cache stores. | Hosted backend behavior and many action inputs are not reproduced. | Paths are workspace-restricted; restored data remains untrusted. Fallback: Use simple names, keys, and paths, then verify hosted behavior. Tests: [engine/internal/artifacts/store_test.go](../engine/internal/artifacts/store_test.go), [engine/internal/caches/store_test.go](../engine/internal/caches/store_test.go) |
| <a id="github-actions-checkout"></a>Actions — actions/checkout (`github.checkout`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | actions/checkout references | Checkout is an explicit successful local emulation because the prepared repository is already mounted. | Fetch depth, ref selection, submodules, credentials, clean behavior, and sparse checkout are not reproduced. | The mounted workspace may be writable depending on run settings. Fallback: Prepare the desired local revision before running Piper. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="github-local-github-actions"></a>Actions — Local GitHub actions (`github.local-action`) | `partial` / `execute` | local JavaScript, composite, and docker action metadata | Supported local JavaScript, composite run steps, and docker:// actions execute locally. | Nested uses steps, Dockerfile builds, action state, and full command protocol are incomplete. | Local actions execute arbitrary repository code. Fallback: Use supported runtimes and run-only composite steps. Tests: [engine/internal/actions/resolver_test.go](../engine/internal/actions/resolver_test.go) |
| <a id="github-remote-github-actions"></a>Actions — Remote GitHub actions (`github.remote-action`) | `requires-consent` / `consent` | owner/repository[/path]@ref references | Remote actions require explicit consent, resolve to a commit, are cached, and emit provenance. | GitHub's download service, token context, and runner command protocol are not reproduced. | Downloaded third-party code executes locally; mutable references are warned. Fallback: Pin a commit SHA, review the source, or replace it with a local action. Tests: [engine/internal/actions/resolver_test.go](../engine/internal/actions/resolver_test.go) |
| <a id="github-setup-node-and-setup-dotnet"></a>Actions — setup-node and setup-dotnet (`github.setup-runtime`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | setup-node and setup-dotnet version inputs | A compatible Node or .NET Docker image is selected when one version can represent the job. | Tool cache, authentication, architecture, caching, and multi-runtime images are not reproduced. | Selected images are pulled and executed locally. Fallback: Use one concrete supported runtime version per job. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/runtime_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/runtime_test.go) |
| <a id="github-unsupported-github-action-runtime"></a>Actions — Unsupported GitHub action runtime (`github.unsupported-action`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | unhandled action references or runtimes are retained | The action fails with a structured support error and is never reported successful. | GitHub may support the action or runtime. | Rejecting unknown runtimes prevents unreviewed fake-success behavior. Fallback: Use a supported action, local command, or hosted CI. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go) |
| <a id="github-reusable-workflows"></a>Composition — Reusable workflows (`github.reusable-workflow`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | workflow_call and jobs.<id>.uses are detected | Reusable workflow execution is rejected before local job steps begin. | GitHub resolves inputs, secrets, nested calls, permissions, and outputs. | Remote reusable workflow code is not downloaded or executed. Fallback: Run the called workflow directly or validate the caller on GitHub. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="github-environment-approval"></a>Deployment — Environment approval (`github.environment`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | job environment name | Environment jobs pause for explicit local approval and use restricted networking. | GitHub environment protection rules, reviewers, secrets, and deployment records are not reproduced. | Local approval is not provider authorization and must not imply production safety. Fallback: Use non-production credentials and validate protections on GitHub. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go) |
| <a id="github-workflow-triggers"></a>Events — Workflow triggers (`github.triggers`) | `validation-only` / `inspect-only` | trigger names and workflow_dispatch input metadata | Triggers are displayed; the user chooses a local event name and inputs. | Piper does not decide whether GitHub would start a workflow. | No GitHub API or credentials are needed for event selection. Fallback: Provide local event/input values and verify actual triggering on GitHub. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="github-job-and-step-if-conditions"></a>Expressions — Job and step if conditions (`github.conditions`) | `partial` / `execute` | if expressions with source location | A documented subset of status, comparison, boolean, string, context, and output expressions is evaluated. | GitHub coercion, functions, object semantics, and context completeness are not fully reproduced. | Evaluation failures reject execution rather than defaulting true. Fallback: Simplify the condition or validate unsupported syntax on GitHub. Tests: [engine/internal/expression/evaluator_test.go](../engine/internal/expression/evaluator_test.go) |
| <a id="github-expression-interpolation"></a>Expressions — Expression interpolation (`github.interpolation`) | `partial` / `execute` | ${{ }} in run, env, paths, outputs, and cache keys used by the runtime | Supported expressions are interpolated immediately before their local use. | Not every GitHub field or expression construct is evaluated. | Interpolated values can affect commands and paths; secrets remain best-effort redacted. Fallback: Use supported contexts or explicit local environment values. Tests: [engine/internal/expression/evaluator_test.go](../engine/internal/expression/evaluator_test.go) |
| <a id="github-continue-on-error"></a>Failure — continue-on-error (`github.continue-on-error`) | `partial` / `execute` | literal step boolean | A failed step may continue the local job and emits an explicit continuation event. | Expression forms and GitHub outcome/conclusion distinctions are incomplete. | Failures remain visible in structured events. Fallback: Use literal booleans and inspect step events. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go) |
| <a id="github-permissions-and-concurrency"></a>Policy — Permissions and concurrency (`github.permissions-concurrency`) | `validation-only` / `inspect-only` | workflow and job keys are detected | The configuration is reported but does not alter local credentials or scheduling. | GitHub token permissions, concurrency groups, and cancellation are not reproduced. | Piper does not mint a GitHub token or imply provider authorization. Fallback: Review the values and validate policy behavior on GitHub. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="github-defaults-run"></a>Runtime — defaults.run (`github.defaults`) | `partial` / `execute` | job working-directory and shell defaults | Working-directory defaults are applied; supported shell selection is honored where available. | Workflow-level defaults and full GitHub shell templates are not reproduced. | Working directories remain restricted to the workspace at execution. Fallback: Declare explicit supported step working directories and shells. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="github-job-container"></a>Runtime — Job container (`github.job-container`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | jobs.<id>.container is detected | GitHub job-container semantics are rejected rather than silently replaced. | GitHub configures container credentials, options, volumes, networking, and shell behavior. | Arbitrary host mounts and options are not accepted. Fallback: Use Piper's selected job image or validate the container job on GitHub. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="github-runs-on-labels"></a>Runtime — runs-on labels (`github.runner`) | `validation-only` / `inspect-only` | scalar and list labels | Requested labels are displayed but do not select a hosted runner image. | GitHub label matching and hosted operating systems are not reproduced. | A Linux local image may run code intended for another platform. Fallback: Select a compatible local image and validate platform-specific jobs on GitHub. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="github-job-services"></a>Runtime — Job services (`github.services`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | service image, environment, ports, and options metadata | Supported service fields use Piper's local service-container emulation; options are displayed but not applied. | GitHub service option parsing and networking differ. | Service images are untrusted and configured ports may be exposed locally. Fallback: Avoid relying on unsupported container options. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/services_integration_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/services_integration_test.go) |
| <a id="github-workflow-and-job-metadata"></a>Syntax — Workflow and job metadata (`github.metadata`) | `supported-local` / `execute` | name, job name, IDs, needs, and source locations | Metadata is represented in the model, graph, and UI. | Display formatting may differ from GitHub. | None beyond parsing untrusted YAML. Fallback: Use standard GitHub workflow metadata. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="github-strategy-matrix"></a>Topology — Strategy matrix (`github.matrix`) | `partial` / `execute` | static dimensions, include, exclude, fail-fast, and max-parallel | Static combinations expand deterministically into selectable local job instances. | Dynamic expressions and every include/exclude semantic are not guaranteed identical. | Expansion limits prevent unbounded local resource use. Fallback: Keep matrices static and below the configured expansion limit. Tests: [engine/internal/pipeline/plan/plan_test.go](../engine/internal/pipeline/plan/plan_test.go) |
| <a id="github-unknown-github-workflow-feature"></a>Validation — Unknown GitHub workflow feature (`github.unknown`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | unknown keys at recognized workflow, job, and step scopes | Unknown executable semantics are surfaced and rejected. | The key may be valid GitHub syntax not yet modeled by Piper. | Rejecting unknown behavior prevents silent success. Fallback: Consult the support reference or validate the feature on GitHub. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="github-workflow-job-and-step-env"></a>Variables — Workflow, job, and step env (`github.env`) | `partial` / `execute` | literal mapping values at workflow, job, and step scope | Environment maps are merged and supported expressions are interpolated before execution. | GitHub coercion, context availability, and all environment-file semantics are incomplete. | Environment values can contain secrets and are passed to job processes. Fallback: Use literal values or supported expressions and avoid relying on unavailable contexts. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/github/provider_test.go), [engine/internal/expression/evaluator_test.go](../engine/internal/expression/evaluator_test.go) |
| <a id="github-step-and-job-outputs"></a>Variables — Step and job outputs (`github.outputs`) | `partial` / `execute` | job output definitions and GITHUB_OUTPUT records | Single-line and multiline step outputs feed steps and needs contexts; job outputs are interpolated after execution. | All environment-file commands, output limits, encoding, and edge cases are incomplete. | Output values are untrusted and secret redaction is best effort. Fallback: Use GITHUB_OUTPUT with simple values and verify complex cases on GitHub. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/runtime_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/runtime_test.go) |

## GitLab CI/CD

| Category / feature | Status / runtime | Parser contract | Local behavior | Hosted difference | Security / fallback / tests |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="gitlab-child-and-multi-project-pipelines"></a>Composition — Child and multi-project pipelines (`gitlab.child-pipeline`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | trigger forms are detected | Triggered pipeline behavior is rejected locally. | GitLab can create child or remote project pipelines with provider-side permissions. | Piper never triggers a remote pipeline by default. Fallback: Open a local child file directly or validate the trigger on GitLab. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-default-inheritance"></a>Composition — default inheritance (`gitlab.default`) | `partial` / `execute` | default image plus visibility of the remaining default mapping | Only the default image is inherited; other default fields are reported as missing local semantics. | GitLab supports broader default inheritance and inherit controls. | Inherited images execute locally; ignored defaults can otherwise change commands, credentials, or cleanup behavior. Fallback: Declare non-image defaults directly on jobs or validate them on GitLab. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-extends-and-hidden-jobs"></a>Composition — extends and hidden jobs (`gitlab.extends`) | `supported-local` / `execute` | single or multiple parents and hidden job definitions | Jobs are resolved through reverse deep merging with cycle detection. | Every inheritance control and GitLab merge nuance is not yet covered. | Resolution is local and does not execute code. Fallback: Use acyclic local templates and inspect the resolved YAML. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/resolver_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/resolver_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-local-includes"></a>Composition — Local includes (`gitlab.include-local`) | `supported-local` / `execute` | string and local include forms with nesting | Local include files are recursively merged with cycle detection before parsing. | All GitLab include merge nuances and integrity metadata are not guaranteed. | Included paths are restricted to the repository workspace. Fallback: Keep local include graphs small and acyclic. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/resolver_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/resolver_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-remote-project-and-template-includes"></a>Composition — Remote, project, and template includes (`gitlab.include-remote`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | non-local include forms are detected and preserved | Piper does not fetch them and rejects execution because the resolved pipeline is incomplete. | GitLab fetches and merges remote/project/template content under provider trust rules. | No remote content is downloaded without a consent-aware resolver. Fallback: Vendor the include locally or validate on GitLab. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/resolver_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/resolver_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-top-level-cache-and-services-defaults"></a>Composition — Top-level cache and services defaults (`gitlab.unsupported-global-policy`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | top-level cache and services keys are detected | Execution is rejected because these global defaults are not applied to jobs. | GitLab propagates deprecated top-level cache and services declarations to jobs. | Silently ignoring global services or cache content could change the code and dependencies used by a job. Fallback: Move cache and services declarations onto each job for local testing. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-environments-and-manual-jobs"></a>Deployment — Environments and manual jobs (`gitlab.environment-manual`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | environment name and when: manual | Piper pauses for explicit local approval and restricts deployment-job networking. | GitLab protected environments, deployment records, and permissions are not reproduced. | Approval is local only and does not authorize a real deployment. Fallback: Use non-production credentials and verify protections on GitLab. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-workflow-rules"></a>Events — workflow: rules (`gitlab.workflow-rules`) | `validation-only` / `inspect-only` | workflow key is detected | Workflow rules are reported but do not decide whether the local pipeline exists. | GitLab can suppress or configure the entire pipeline before jobs are created. | No GitLab API or credentials are used. Fallback: Choose a local event explicitly and validate pipeline creation on GitLab. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-job-rules-only-and-except"></a>Expressions — Job rules, only, and except (`gitlab.rules`) | `partial` / `execute` | first matching simple rule, branch/tag forms, regex, and local changed-file patterns | A documented subset is converted to a local job condition. | Rule ordering, attributes, variable expansion, exists, compare_to, and many ref forms are incomplete. | Evaluation errors fail rather than default true. Fallback: Use simple explicit conditions and verify complex rules on GitLab. Tests: [engine/internal/expression/evaluator_test.go](../engine/internal/expression/evaluator_test.go), [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-allow-failure"></a>Failure — allow_failure (`gitlab.allow-failure`) | `partial` / `execute` | literal job boolean | A failed local job can be converted to a successful scheduler result with an explicit event. | Exit-code forms and GitLab warning status semantics are incomplete. | The original failure remains visible in events. Fallback: Use literal booleans and inspect job_failure_allowed events. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-dependencies-resource-group-coverage-retry-and-timeout"></a>Failure — dependencies, resource_group, coverage, retry, and timeout (`gitlab.unsupported-job-policy`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | these job keys are detected | Execution is rejected instead of silently ignoring job data flow, locking, reporting, retry, or timeout behavior. | GitLab implements each feature in its runner and scheduler. | Rejecting ignored policies prevents misleading success and uncontrolled execution. Fallback: Remove the feature for a local-only fixture or validate the job on GitLab. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-images-and-runner-tags"></a>Runtime — Images and runner tags (`gitlab.image-tags`) | `partial` / `execute` | root/default/job image and job tags | The selected image is used locally; tags are display metadata. | Runner selection, entrypoints, credentials, and executor behavior differ. | Images are pulled and executed locally. Fallback: Use a Bash-capable trusted image. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-script-before-script-and-after-script"></a>Runtime — script, before_script, and after_script (`gitlab.scripts`) | `partial` / `execute` | global and job script blocks | Script blocks become ordered Bash steps; after_script currently follows normal success-gated step behavior. | GitLab after_script isolation, timeout, failure, and cancellation semantics are incomplete. | Scripts are arbitrary code with configured workspace access. Fallback: Keep local scripts Bash-compatible and verify after_script edge cases on GitLab. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-services"></a>Runtime — Services (`gitlab.services`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | image/name, alias/aliases, and variables | Services use Piper's local service-container network. | GitLab executor networking and service command/entrypoint behavior differ. | Service images are untrusted local code. Fallback: Use explicit aliases and trusted images. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/services_integration_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/services_integration_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-artifacts-and-cache"></a>Storage — Artifacts and cache (`gitlab.artifacts-cache`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | common paths, names, keys, restore behavior, policy, when, and expiry metadata | Common declarations use Piper-managed local stores. | Reports, exclusions, expiry enforcement, scopes, and cloud backends differ. | Paths are workspace-restricted and stored content remains untrusted. Fallback: Use simple paths and inspect local records. Tests: [engine/internal/artifacts/store_test.go](../engine/internal/artifacts/store_test.go), [engine/internal/caches/store_test.go](../engine/internal/caches/store_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-jobs-stages-and-needs"></a>Syntax — Jobs, stages, and needs (`gitlab.metadata`) | `partial` / `execute` | top-level jobs, stages/types, needs, and implicit stage edges | Jobs and explicit needs are normalized; missing needs use an adjacent-stage approximation. | GitLab stage scheduling, optional needs, and all DAG forms are not fully reproduced. | No pipeline code runs during graph construction. Fallback: Prefer explicit simple needs for deterministic local behavior. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-parallel-jobs-and-matrix"></a>Topology — parallel jobs and matrix (`gitlab.parallel`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | parallel is detected | Parallel expansion is rejected before execution. | GitLab creates numbered or matrix job instances and maps dependencies accordingly. | Rejecting prevents accidental unbounded or incomplete execution. Fallback: Create explicit local jobs or validate on GitLab. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-unknown-gitlab-job-feature"></a>Validation — Unknown GitLab job feature (`gitlab.unknown`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | unknown keys at recognized workflow and job scopes | Unknown semantics are surfaced and rejected. | The key may be valid GitLab syntax not yet modeled. | Rejecting unknown behavior prevents silent success. Fallback: Consult the support reference or validate on GitLab. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="gitlab-global-and-job-variables"></a>Variables — Global and job variables (`gitlab.variables`) | `partial` / `execute` | literal global and job variable mappings | Variables are merged into the local job environment. | Recursive expansion, protected/masked variables, file variables, and full precedence are incomplete. | Values are passed to job processes; user secrets are only best-effort redacted. Fallback: Use explicit literal variables and verify precedence on GitLab. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/gitlab/provider_test.go) |

## Azure Pipelines

| Category / feature | Status / runtime | Parser contract | Local behavior | Hosted difference | Security / fallback / tests |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| <a id="azure-parameters-and-compile-time-expressions"></a>Composition — Parameters and compile-time expressions (`azure.parameters`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | parameters are detected | Execution is rejected because parameterized configuration is not resolved. | Azure expands typed parameters and compile-time template expressions before runtime. | Rejecting avoids executing an incorrectly resolved pipeline. Fallback: Create a resolved local fixture or validate on Azure. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-repository-pipeline-package-and-container-resources"></a>Composition — Repository, pipeline, package, and container resources (`azure.resources`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | resources is detected | Execution is rejected because referenced resources are not resolved. | Azure authenticates and fetches configured external resources. | Piper does not use service connections or fetch remote resources. Fallback: Vendor required local content or validate on Azure. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-templates-and-root-extends"></a>Composition — Templates and root extends (`azure.templates`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | step template and root extends references are detected | Execution is rejected because templates are not expanded. | Azure resolves nested step, job, stage, variable, and extends templates. | Remote template code is not downloaded or executed. Fallback: Create a resolved local fixture or validate on Azure. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-deployment-jobs"></a>Deployment — Deployment jobs (`azure.deployment`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | deployment job, environment, and runOnce deploy steps | Deployment jobs use a local approval gate and internal-only network. | Azure environments, checks, service connections, strategies, and deployment history are not reproduced. | Approval is local and must not be treated as production authorization. Fallback: Use non-production credentials and validate deployment controls on Azure. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-triggers-prs-and-schedules"></a>Events — Triggers, PRs, and schedules (`azure.triggers`) | `validation-only` / `inspect-only` | trigger, pr, and schedules keys | Trigger metadata is displayed; the user supplies a local build reason. | Piper does not decide whether Azure would queue a pipeline. | No Azure service connection is contacted. Fallback: Select a local event and verify queueing behavior on Azure. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-job-and-step-condition"></a>Expressions — Job and step condition (`azure.conditions`) | `partial` / `execute` | condition strings with source location | A subset of status, equality, and boolean functions is evaluated. | Compile-time expressions, all runtime functions, dependency outputs, and type coercion are incomplete. | Evaluation failures reject execution rather than default true. Fallback: Use supported condition functions or verify on Azure. Tests: [engine/internal/expression/evaluator_test.go](../engine/internal/expression/evaluator_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-continueonerror"></a>Failure — continueOnError (`azure.continue-on-error`) | `partial` / `execute` | literal job and step booleans | Step and job failures can continue locally and emit explicit continuation/allowed-failure events. | Azure result states and expression forms are incomplete. | Original failures remain visible in structured events. Fallback: Use literal booleans and inspect events. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/executor_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-timeoutinminutes-and-cancellation-timeout"></a>Failure — timeoutInMinutes and cancellation timeout (`azure.timeout`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | job timeout keys are detected | Provider timeout declarations are rejected because only Piper's explicit local timeout settings are enforced. | Azure applies job and cancellation timeouts with agent semantics. | Ignoring a timeout could allow unintended long-running code, so Piper rejects it. Fallback: Configure Piper's local timeout explicitly and verify provider timeout behavior on Azure. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-checkout-step"></a>Runtime — checkout step (`azure.checkout`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | checkout steps | Checkout is an explicit successful local emulation because the prepared repository is already mounted. | Repository resources, clean, fetch, submodules, credentials, and persistence are not reproduced. | The mounted workspace may be writable. Fallback: Prepare the desired local revision before running Piper. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-job-container"></a>Runtime — Job container (`azure.job-container`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | job container is detected | Azure job-container semantics are rejected. | Azure configures container resources, endpoints, options, mounts, and agent initialization. | Arbitrary mounts and container options are not accepted. Fallback: Use Piper's selected job image or validate on Azure. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-pool-and-vmimage"></a>Runtime — pool and vmImage (`azure.pool`) | `validation-only` / `inspect-only` | pool scalar, name, and vmImage | The requested pool/image is displayed but does not select a Microsoft-hosted image. | Azure agent demands, capabilities, operating systems, and hosted images are not reproduced. | A Linux local image may run code intended for another platform. Fallback: Choose a compatible local image and validate platform-specific work on Azure. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-bash-script-cmdline-and-powershell"></a>Runtime — Bash, script, CmdLine, and PowerShell (`azure.scripts`) | `partial` / `execute` | bash/script/pwsh/powershell steps and common script tasks | Scripts run through Bash or pwsh when available; CmdLine is approximated with Bash. | Windows cmd.exe, task wrappers, profiles, preferences, and agent logging commands differ. | Scripts are arbitrary code with workspace access. Fallback: Use explicit Bash or ensure pwsh exists in the image. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-service-containers"></a>Runtime — Service containers (`azure.services`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | service image, environment, and ports | Services use Piper's local per-job Docker network. | Azure container resources, endpoints, options, and agent networking differ. | Service images execute locally and ports may be exposed. Fallback: Use trusted images and simple service configuration. Tests: [engine/internal/executor/docker/services_integration_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/services_integration_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-workingdirectory"></a>Runtime — workingDirectory (`azure.working-directory`) | `supported-local` / `execute` | step workingDirectory | Relative working directories are resolved below the prepared workspace. | Azure task-specific path defaults may differ. | Workspace escape attempts are rejected. Fallback: Use repository-relative paths. Tests: [engine/internal/workspace/workspace_test.go](../engine/internal/workspace/workspace_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-pipelines-stages-jobs-and-dependencies"></a>Syntax — Pipelines, stages, jobs, and dependencies (`azure.metadata`) | `partial` / `execute` | root steps/jobs, stages, displayName, dependsOn, and stage ordering | Azure structures are normalized into jobs and deterministic dependency edges. | Stage conditions, approvals, server jobs, and all dependency expressions are incomplete. | No pipeline code runs during normalization. Fallback: Use agent jobs with explicit dependencies. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-script-and-runtime-setup-tasks"></a>Tasks — Script and runtime setup tasks (`azure.task-runtime`) | `partial` / `execute` | Bash, PowerShell, CmdLine, NodeTool, and UseNode handlers | Known tasks map to local shell execution or Node image selection. | Task versions, inputs, tool caches, logging commands, and platform behavior are incomplete. | Task scripts and selected images execute locally. Fallback: Use documented inputs or replace the task with an explicit local command. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go), [engine/internal/executor/docker/runtime_test.go](../engine/internal/executor/docker/runtime_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-artifact-and-cache-tasks"></a>Tasks — Artifact and cache tasks (`azure.task-storage`) | `emulated` / `emulate` | PublishBuildArtifacts, DownloadBuildArtifacts, and Cache inputs used locally | Known handlers use Piper-managed artifact and cache stores. | Pipeline artifact services, scopes, retention, and many task inputs are not reproduced. | Paths are workspace-restricted and restored data remains untrusted. Fallback: Use simple names, keys, and paths, then validate hosted behavior. Tests: [engine/internal/artifacts/store_test.go](../engine/internal/artifacts/store_test.go), [engine/internal/caches/store_test.go](../engine/internal/caches/store_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-unknown-azure-task"></a>Tasks — Unknown Azure task (`azure.unknown-task`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | unknown task names and versions are retained | Unknown tasks fail with a structured support error. | Azure may provide the task through its marketplace or built-in catalog. | Piper never fakes successful execution of an unknown task. Fallback: Use a supported task or replace it with an explicit local command. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-matrix-strategy"></a>Topology — Matrix strategy (`azure.matrix`) | `partial` / `execute` | named static matrix legs and maxParallel | Named legs expand deterministically into local job instances. | Dynamic strategy expressions and every Azure scheduling nuance are incomplete. | Expansion limits constrain local resource use. Fallback: Keep matrix legs static and below the expansion limit. Tests: [engine/internal/pipeline/plan/plan_test.go](../engine/internal/pipeline/plan/plan_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-unknown-azure-pipeline-feature"></a>Validation — Unknown Azure pipeline feature (`azure.unknown`) | `unsupported` / `reject` | unknown keys at recognized workflow, job, and step scopes | Unknown semantics are surfaced and rejected. | The key may be valid Azure syntax not yet modeled. | Rejecting unknown behavior prevents silent success. Fallback: Consult the support reference or validate on Azure. Tests: [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
| <a id="azure-root-stage-job-and-step-variables"></a>Variables — Root, stage, job, and step variables (`azure.variables`) | `partial` / `execute` | literal maps, name/value lists, and step env | Literal variables are merged and supported macros/expressions are evaluated at local runtime. | Variable groups, secret variables, precedence, compile-time insertion, and logging commands are incomplete. | Values are passed to processes and may contain secrets. Fallback: Use explicit local values and verify complex precedence on Azure. Tests: [engine/internal/expression/evaluator_test.go](../engine/internal/expression/evaluator_test.go), [engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go](../engine/internal/providers/azure/provider_test.go) |
